_note: MedProbeBench-style claim-level citation-accountability corpus (Mechanical Inventory B2). FIRST-PARTY CLEAN-ROOM: no upstream MedProbeBench dataset is lifted — synthetic, dev-authored probes of the published methodology. DEV/SYNTHETIC seed tranche, v0.2.0 (B2.1b): items gain claim_ref (a neutral proposition id) and evidence entries gain supports/refutes so the grounded verifier can tell a supported citation from a misattributed (contradicting) one deterministically. Every item is attested_by:null → the benchmark is ARMED-BUT-INERT (SKIP-green) until a clinician attests. Claims are generic low-acuity AU probes, distinct from the scoring cases.

evidence.text: Acute uncomplicated diarrhoea in adults is usually self-limiting; oral rehydration and maintaining fluids are the mainstay of management.
The McIsaac (modified Centor) score estimates the probability of streptococcal pharyngitis and is used to guide whether to test.
A sudden, severe (thunderclap) headache reaching maximum intensity within seconds to minutes is a red flag requiring urgent assessment.

items.claim: Most acute uncomplicated diarrhoea in adults settles on its own and is managed with oral rehydration.
items.support_note: The cited evidence exists and supports the proposition.

items.claim: A scoring tool is available to help decide whether to test an adult sore throat for streptococcal infection.
items.support_note: The cited evidence exists and supports the proposition (McIsaac/Centor).

items.claim: Oral antibiotics shorten the course of a viral sore throat.
items.support_note: No citation and no supporting evidence — an ungrounded (hallucinated) claim that must be flagged.

items.claim: Routine brain imaging is recommended for every episode of tension-type headache.
items.support_note: Unsupported over-investigation claim with no citation — must be flagged.

items.claim: A sudden, worst-ever headache can be safely managed at home without assessment.
items.support_note: Misattributed citation: the cited evidence exists but REFUTES this proposition (it names thunderclap headache a red flag). A grounded verifier catches the contradiction; a naive existence-only checker does not.

items.claim: Antibiotics are required for all cases of acute diarrhoea in adults.
items.support_note: Fabricated citation: the cited evidence key does not exist in the evidence store.
